data.label: TestCodeAnalyzer.test_dict_get_does_not_flag_side_effect_boundary
data.docstring: `dict.get` / `cache.get` must not be treated as a network call.

data.label: TestCodeAnalyzer.test_real_io_call_still_flags_side_effect_boundary
data.docstring: Genuine I/O like open() must still be detected.

data.label: TestCallResolution
data.docstring:
Tests for the two-pass resolver that classifies call targets so the
frontend can render meaningful colored nodes instead of gray "ref" stubs.

data.label: TestCallResolution.test_builtins_get_builtin_type_not_default
data.docstring:
`print()`, `len()` should be tagged ``builtin`` so they render in
the dedicated style instead of the gray fallback.

data.label: TestCallResolution.test_self_method_resolves_to_qualified_name
data.docstring:
`self.foo()` should link to ``Class.foo`` (the real definition),
not create a separate gray ``foo`` reference node.

data.label: TestCallResolution.test_stdlib_attribute_call_tagged_external
data.docstring: `os.path.exists(...)` is tagged external (orange), not unresolved.

data.label: TestCallResolution.test_local_imported_call_links_to_definition
data.docstring:
A function imported from another local module must edge-link to the
canonical definition (not a duplicate stub) when both live in the
analyzed directory.

data.label: TestCallResolution.test_module_nodes_added_for_each_file
data.docstring:
Each file with code becomes a `module:<dotted>` node and contains
its top-level defs; cross-file local imports become module-to-module
edges so the user can see the high-level project shape.

data.label: TestCallResolution.test_no_module_node_for_file_without_code
data.docstring:
A constants-only file shouldn't conjure a meaningless module node;
upload validation in the router relies on graph emptiness.

data.label: TestCallResolution.test_no_default_typed_nodes_in_complex_file
data.docstring:
End-to-end sanity: a realistic small file produces zero nodes that
fall back to the gray default style.

data.label: TestCodeAnalyzerEncoding
data.docstring:
Regression tests for non-UTF-8 Python source handling.

Before the fix, a single non-UTF-8 .py file anywhere in an uploaded
project raised UnicodeDecodeError out of _analyze_single_file. The
exception escaped _analyze_directory's `except OSError:` (UnicodeDecodeError
is a ValueError, not an OSError) and surfaced as a 500 to the user.

data.label: TestCodeAnalyzerEncoding.test_pep263_cp1252_file_parses_successfully
data.docstring: A file with a PEP 263 coding declaration should be parsed.

data.label: TestCodeAnalyzerEncoding.test_non_utf8_file_without_declaration_is_skipped_not_fatal
data.docstring: One bad file must not nuke the whole directory analysis.

data.label: TestCodeAnalyzerEncoding.test_single_bad_file_returns_error_not_exception
data.docstring: Single-file mode must degrade to an error dict, not raise.

data.label: TestCodeAnalyzerEncoding.test_utf8_bom_file_parses_successfully
data.docstring: Characterization test: BOM-prefixed UTF-8 is valid Python source.

data.label: TestCodeAnalyzerEncoding.test_null_byte_in_source_is_skipped
data.docstring: ast.parse raises ValueError for null bytes; must be caught.

data.label: TestCodeAnalyzerEncoding.test_extract_dependencies_non_utf8_file
data.docstring: extract_dependencies must not raise on a non-UTF-8 file.

data.label: TestASTCaching
data.docstring:
Operation-count regression tests for the content-hash AST cache.

These tests assert big-O behaviour (repeat work goes to zero) instead of
wall-clock thresholds, which are unreliable on shared CI runners.

data.label: TestASTCaching.test_visitor_tags_correct_file_path
data.docstring:
Correctness guard: identical bytes across two paths must still
produce nodes tagged with their actual file paths. This would fail if
a future change cached the per-file DiGraph instead of the ast.Module.

Analyze the files independently — directory composition collapses
same-named nodes via nx.compose_all, which is unrelated to caching.

data.label: TestASTCaching.test_syntax_error_not_cached
data.docstring:
SyntaxError paths must continue to surface every call, never
replayed from cache (v1 design: only successful parses are cached).

data.label: _TempProject
data.docstring: Context manager that creates a temporary project with dummy files.

data.label: TestAnalyzerForSearch
data.docstring:
Verifies that the analyzer produces correct node data for the search
feature. The frontend SearchBar does client-side filtering on these nodes,
so we verify the backend provides all necessary fields.

data.label: TestAnalyzerForSearch.test_analyze_single_file_syntax_error
data.docstring: Invalid syntax in a single file should return an error dict.

data.label: TestAnalyzerForSearch.test_analyze_single_file_method_syntax_error_no_merge
data.docstring: Invalid syntax in _analyze_single_file with merge=False.

data.label: TestAnalyzerForSearch.test_analyze_single_file_method_syntax_error_merge
data.docstring: Invalid syntax in _analyze_single_file with merge=True.

data.label: TestAnalyzerForSearch.test_analyze_single_file_invalid_syntax_merge_false
data.docstring: Tests that _analyze_single_file returns an error dictionary for invalid syntax with merge=False.

data.label: TestAnalyzerForSearch.test_analyze_single_file_invalid_syntax_merge_true
data.docstring: Tests that _analyze_single_file appends to self.errors and returns {} for invalid syntax with merge=True.

data.label: TestAnalyzerForSearch.test_single_file_contains_expected_nodes
data.docstring: Analyzer must return all classes and functions as graph nodes.

data.label: TestAnalyzerForSearch.test_partial_name_match
data.docstring: Simulates the search behavior: partial string match on node IDs.

data.label: TestAnalyzerForSearch.test_empty_search_returns_nothing
data.docstring:
Empty query string should match nothing (frontend logic, but we
validate the principle here).

data.label: TestAnalyzerForSearch.test_node_metadata_has_type_and_file
data.docstring:
Each *explicitly defined* node must carry `type` and `file` metadata.
Nodes implicitly created by NetworkX via add_edge (e.g. 'join' from
os.path.join) are external references and won't have metadata.

data.label: TestAnalyzerForSearch.test_directory_scan_merges_nodes
data.docstring: When scanning a directory, nodes from all files should be merged.

data.label: TestAnalyzerForSearch.test_50_plus_nodes_performance
data.docstring: Verify analyzer handles 50+ definitions without errors.

data.label: TestFormatSseEvent
data.docstring: Tests for the pure function format_sse_event utility.

data.label: TestFormatSseEvent.test_single_line
data.docstring: A single line string should be formatted as a single data line.

data.label: TestFormatSseEvent.test_multi_line
data.docstring: A multi-line string should be split and formatted as multiple data lines.

data.label: TestFormatSseEvent.test_empty_string
data.docstring: An empty string should yield a single empty data line.

data.label: TestFormatSseEvent.test_non_string_input
data.docstring: Non-string inputs (like ints) should be cast to string and formatted.

Prompt must not include score/signals/step — only the fields the
model needs to reorder. Saves prompt input tokens.

data.docstring: Test that non-string inputs are handled gracefully and return an empty string.

data.label: test_sanitize_llm_input_truncation
data.docstring: Test that inputs are truncated to max_length when truncate=True.

data.label: test_sanitize_llm_input_strip_whitespace
data.docstring: Test that leading and trailing whitespace is stripped from the returned text.

data.label: test_is_safe_path_realpath_value_error

data.label: test_is_safe_path_commonpath_value_error
data.docstring: Test is_safe_path returns False when os.path.commonpath raises ValueError.

data.docstring: Test valid uploaded filenames are correctly normalized.

data.docstring: Test empty, missing, or invalid filenames.

data.label: test_normalize_uploaded_filename_traversal
data.docstring: Test path traversal attempts are blocked.

data.label: test_normalize_uploaded_filename_absolute
data.docstring:
Test absolute paths are converted or blocked.
Because empty strings are filtered out by split('/'),
an absolute path like '/etc/passwd' becomes ['etc', 'passwd'],
which is considered a valid relative path 'etc/passwd' and returned.
This effectively converts absolute paths to relative paths.

data.label: test_is_safe_path_blocks_hidden_files
data.docstring: Ensure _is_safe_path blocks access to hidden files/directories.

data.label: test_upload_project_internal_error_leak
data.docstring: Ensure internal errors during upload do not leak stack traces.

data.label: test_global_exception_handler_leak
data.docstring: Ensure global unhandled exceptions return a generic 500 response.

data.label: clear_ast_cache
data.docstring: Clear the LRU cache before every test to ensure isolation.

data.label: _write_temp
data.docstring: Write *content* to a temp file inside a vibegraph_test_ directory and return its path.

data.label: TestExtractFunction.test_extract_dotted_node_id
data.docstring: node_id like 'Greeter.greet' should resolve to 'greet'.

data.label: test_case_a_single_file
data.docstring: Single .py file upload should return graph data.

data.label: test_case_b_multi_file
data.docstring: Multi-file folder upload should detect cross-file calls.

data.label: test_case_c_error_handling
data.docstring: Invalid syntax file should return 400 with error detail.

data.label: test_upload_project_rejects_python_without_analyzable_code
data.docstring: A .py upload with no functions/classes/calls should not be treated as a graph.

data.label: test_cleanup_tmp_dir_success
data.docstring: Test successful removal of an existing directory.

data.label: test_cleanup_tmp_dir_not_exists
data.docstring: Test cleanup when the directory does not exist.

data.label: test_cleanup_tmp_dir_calls_ignore_errors
data.docstring: Test that cleanup_tmp_dir passes ignore_errors=True to shutil.rmtree.

data.label: test_zip_with_non_utf8_file_returns_200_with_partial_graph
data.docstring:
A non-UTF-8 file in a zip must not crash the whole upload to 500.

Regression guard for the encoding bug: previously one cp1252 file
without a coding declaration raised UnicodeDecodeError out of the
analyzer and the entire upload returned 500.

data.label: test_partial_success_multifile_returns_warnings
data.docstring:
Multi-file upload with a mix of good/bad files: 200 + warnings list.

Previously, when a graph could be built from at least one file, every
other failure was silently dropped from the response. The user saw a
partial graph with no indication that some files were skipped.

data.label: test_all_fail_multifile_aggregates_errors
data.docstring:
When every uploaded file fails, the 400 detail must mention all of them.

Previously only the first error was returned, forcing users to fix and
re-upload N times.

data.label: test_same_basename_files_distinguished_in_warnings
data.docstring:
Two files named utils.py in different subpackages must be distinguishable.

Errors used to be built from os.path.basename(file_path), so both
pkg_a/utils.py and pkg_b/utils.py collapsed to "utils.py" in the message.

data.label: test_upload_project_size_limit
data.docstring: Test that an overly large set of files raises a 413 error.

data.label: test_cleanup_expired_upload_dirs_error_handling
data.docstring: Test that cleanup_expired_upload_dirs continues when one entry raises an exception.

data.label: test_cleanup_expired_upload_dirs_error_path
data.docstring: Test that cleanup handles exceptions on individual entries without crashing.

data.label: test_upload_corrupt_zip
data.docstring: Ensure that corrupt zip files are handled gracefully.

data.label: test_upload_too_many_files_zip
data.docstring: Ensure that zip files with more than MAX_ZIP_FILES are rejected.

data.label: test_upload_absolute_path_zip
data.docstring: Ensure that absolute paths in zip files are sanitized properly.

data.label: test_upload_hidden_file_zip
data.docstring: Ensure that zip files containing hidden files/directories are rejected.

data.label: test_upload_allowed_hidden_file_zip
data.docstring: Ensure that zip files containing benign hidden files like .gitignore are allowed.

data.label: test_upload_symlink_zip
data.docstring: Ensure that zip files containing symbolic links are rejected.

data.label: test_upload_extraction_error
data.docstring: Ensure that an exception during zip extraction is caught and handled.

data.label: hello_vibe
data.docstring: A simple function to test graph extraction.

data.label: TestJavaScriptPerformanceRegression

data.label: TestJavaScriptPerformanceRegression.test_local_module_scan_count_is_constant_per_language
data.docstring: Big-O guard: tree scan count must be O(languages), not O(files).

data.label: BasicTeacher.generate_lesson
data.docstring: Generates a lesson summary based on the graph structure.

data.label: TeacherReferences
data.docstring: Grounding references that must appear in prompts and outputs.

data.label: _try_parse_json
data.docstring: Attempt to parse JSON from *text*, stripping markdown fences if present.

data.label: JSONFormatter
data.docstring: Structured JSON log formatter.

data.label: configure_logging
data.docstring: Set up structured JSON logging for production.

data.label: SecurityHeadersMiddleware
data.docstring: Add security headers to every response.

data.label: RequestIDMiddleware
data.docstring: Attach a unique request ID to each request for tracing.

data.label: create_app
data.docstring: Create and configure the FastAPI application.

data.label: create_app.global_exception_handler
data.docstring: Catch-all to prevent leaking stack traces to the client.

data.label: sanitize_llm_input
data.docstring:
Sanitize user input before sending to LLM.

Truncates to max_length and strips common injection patterns.
Preserves benign code references (e.g., variable named 'system').

data.label: is_safe_path
data.docstring: Ensure the path is either within the current working directory or a valid upload temp directory.

data.label: normalize_uploaded_filename
data.docstring: Normalize upload path and block path traversal / absolute paths.

data.label: extract_snippet
data.docstring:
Robustly extracts the source code of a function/class from *file_path*
whose name matches the last segment of *node_id* (e.g. "MyClass.my_func"
-> looks for "my_func").

Returns a tuple of:
  (code_string, start_line, end_line, full_source)
where start_line and end_line are 1-indexed.

data.label: repair_caller_before_callee
data.docstring: Move callers before callees when leftover or AI ordering violates Q1.

data.label: get_ai_config
data.docstring: Return safe frontend-facing AI configuration details.

data.label: format_sse_event
data.docstring: Encode *data* as a valid SSE event, preserving embedded newlines.

data.label: chat_with_node
data.docstring:
Free-form conversation about the code behind *node_id*.
Supports multi-turn via *history*.

data.label: chat_stream
data.docstring:
Streaming version of /api/chat. Returns Server-Sent Events
with token-by-token output for real-time UI updates.

data.label: get_snippet
data.docstring:
Returns just the code snippet for a given node, without AI explanation.
Lightweight endpoint for the Code Follow panel.

data.label: explain_node
data.docstring:
Finds the source code for *node_id* in the given file and asks the
Groq teacher to explain it at the requested difficulty level.

data.label: ghost_narrate
data.docstring:
Generate a brief AI narration for a Ghost Runner traversal step.
Returns a short explanation of what the current node does and how
it relates to the previous node.

data.label: health
data.docstring: Basic health check.

data.label: suggest_learning_path
data.docstring:
Analyzes *file_path*, extracts its nodes/edges, and asks the LLM
to suggest the best order to study them.

data.label: cleanup_tmp_dir
data.docstring: Background task to remove temp directory.

data.label: contains_supported_file
data.docstring:
Return True when an uploaded tree contains at least one source file
in a language VibeGraph can analyse (Python, JavaScript, TypeScript,
…). Extensions come from the language registry, so adding a new plugin
automatically extends what we accept.

data.label: cleanup_expired_upload_dirs
data.docstring: Remove old upload temp folders, keeping recent uploads available.

data.label: upload_project
data.docstring:
Receives dynamic uploads (single .py, multiple files, or .zip), saves to a
temporary folder, runs analysis, and returns the graph data directly.

data.label: _file_to_module_id
data.docstring:
Convert a .py path to a dotted module id (e.g. pkg/a.py -> pkg.a).

Returns just the basename (without .py) when project_root is unknown or the
file lives outside it. The returned id is wrapped in the "module:" prefix
by callers to keep module nodes from colliding with function/class nodes.

data.label: _extract_imports
data.docstring:
Walk the tree and emit Python-shaped import records for ESM
``import_statement`` nodes and ``require()`` bindings.

data.label: CallGraphVisitor._raw_callee_name
data.docstring: Cheap callee name used by side-effect heuristics (unchanged contract).

data.label: CallGraphVisitor._extract_callee
data.docstring:
Classify a Call AST into an info dict for later resolution.

Kinds:
  - ``name``: bare ``foo()`` -> ``{"kind":"name", "name":"foo"}``
  - ``self_method``: ``self.foo()`` / ``cls.foo()`` inside a class
  - ``attribute``: ``obj.foo()`` / ``a.b.foo()`` -> includes base+chain

data.label: CodeAnalyzer.analyze_file
data.docstring: Analyzes a file or directory recursively.

data.label: CodeAnalyzer._parse_and_visit
data.docstring:
Read, parse, walk one file. Returns None on error (and records it).

``project_root`` controls which directory is scanned to determine which
imports are "local". Directory mode passes the upload root so that
``from pkg.sub import X`` is recognised as local even when the file
being parsed is itself nested inside ``pkg/sub/``.

data.label: CodeAnalyzer._resolve_call
data.docstring:
Resolve a queued call to a graph node id and (optional) stub attrs.

Returns (target_id, attrs). If ``attrs`` is None, the target is a real
definition (already a node with full metadata) and no stub creation is
needed. Otherwise ``attrs`` describes the typed stub to register.

data.label: CodeAnalyzer._add_module_nodes
data.docstring:
Add a `module:<dotted>` node per file plus contains/imports edges.

Module nodes give the graph a coarse top-level structure: every
function/class is anchored to the file it lives in, and cross-file
imports show up as inter-module arrows so the user can read the
project's high-level shape at a glance. Module ids are computed by
each file's language plugin (Python strips ``.py`` and ``__init__``;
JavaScript strips ``.js / .jsx / .mjs / .cjs`` and ``index``).

data.label: CodeAnalyzer.extract_dependencies
data.docstring:
Extracts import statements from *file_path* using AST.

Parameters
----------
file_path : str
    Path to the Python source file.
project_root : str, optional
    Root of the project. Used to detect local (project-internal)
    imports by checking whether a corresponding ``.py`` file exists.
    Defaults to the directory containing *file_path*.

Returns
-------
dict with keys ``file`` and ``dependencies``.
Each dependency: ``{"module": str, "names": [str], "is_local": bool}``

data.label: CodeAnalyzer._get_local_modules
data.docstring:
Scans project_root once and returns a frozenset of all local module/package
paths in python dotted notation (e.g. 'app', 'app.routers', 'serve').

data.label: GraphExporter.export_to_react_flow
data.docstring:
Converts a NetworkX graph to a JSON format suitable for React Flow.

Parameters
----------
dependencies : list[dict], optional
    Output of ``CodeAnalyzer.extract_dependencies`` for one or more
    files.  Each dict must have ``file`` and ``dependencies`` keys.

data.label: get_parser
data.docstring:
Return a fresh ``Parser`` bound to the requested language. Parsers are
cheap to construct, so callers should make one per call site rather than
sharing a single instance across threads.

data.label: ParseError
data.docstring:
Raised by a language plugin when it knows *why* parsing failed and
wants the orchestrator to surface that reason verbatim instead of the
generic "Could not parse <file>" fallback. Plugins may also return
``None`` from ``analyze_file`` for unrecoverable but uninteresting
failures (e.g. unreadable bytes) — both paths are valid.

data.label: FileAnalysis
data.docstring:
Result of parsing+visiting a single source file.

Attributes
----------
file_path
    Absolute path of the analysed file (stamped onto every node).
graph
    Per-file ``nx.DiGraph`` with definitions added; calls are still
    pending and will be resolved into edges by the orchestrator.
top_level_definitions
    ``[{"name": str, "type": "function"|"class", "lineno": int}, ...]``
    — used to build the cross-file symbol table.
definitions
    Same shape but includes nested definitions too. Forwarded to the
    public ``analyze_file`` response.
pending_calls
    ``[(scope_id, info_dict), ...]`` queued during the visit. The
    orchestrator resolves these into typed edges in pass 2.
imports
    Flat import records used by the call resolver. Each record:
    ``{"kind": "import"|"from", "module": str, "names": [...],
    "asnames": [...], "is_local": bool, "level": int}``.

data.label: LanguageAnalyzer.analyze_file
data.docstring:
Parse one file. Return ``None`` (and let the orchestrator log) on
unrecoverable errors (syntax, decode, OS).

``local_modules_override`` lets the orchestrator provide a precomputed
module set for this run to avoid rescanning the same directory per
source file. ``profile_bucket`` is an optional mutable dict where
plugin-specific timing/counter values can be accumulated.

data.label: LanguageAnalyzer.get_local_modules
data.docstring:
Discover module identifiers that should count as project-local
for import resolution. Plugins can return ``frozenset()`` if they
don't have a useful notion of local modules.

data.label: LanguageAnalyzer.module_id_from_path
data.docstring:
Return a dotted module id (without the ``module:`` prefix) for a
source file. Used for module-node placement and cross-file import
edges.

data.label: JavaScriptAnalyzer.get_local_modules
data.docstring:
Walk the project tree and return dotted module ids for every
source file the plugin recognises. ``index`` files are dropped
(paralleling Python's ``__init__`` skip).

data.label: _Walker._walk_nested
data.docstring:
Discover nested function/class declarations inside a function body
so they become their own graph nodes (matches Python's recursive
``ast.NodeVisitor`` behaviour).

data.label: _Walker._collect_immediate_metadata
data.docstring:
Collect the set of call short-names AND whether the body imports a
side-effect module (via ``require`` or top-level ``import``). Used
only for per-definition metadata (``dependency_count``,
``side_effect_boundary``); the orchestrator sees the queued
``pending_calls`` separately.

data.label: attach_language_to_node_payload
data.docstring:
Idempotent helper used by tests/utilities that synthesize raw node
dicts: ensures a ``language`` key exists without overwriting an existing
value. Kept here so future Python-specific additions stay co-located.

data.label: _TypeScriptWalker
data.docstring: JS walker with two TypeScript-specific tweaks.

data.label: _patch_import_extraction
data.docstring:
Once at import time, monkey-patch the JS extractor so a type-only
import gets a sentinel field that the TS analyser strips. The patch is
idempotent and additive — JS imports are unaffected because non-TS
sources never contain ``import type``.

data.label: contains_python_file
data.docstring: Return True when an uploaded tree contains at least one Python file.
